finding.recommendation: Proceed with the pass-1 two-skill architecture only if the migration is gated by mode-aware parity, not just path rewrites. The merged public advisor identity can be `deep-loop-workflows`, but every command, agent, advisor replay, reducer, governance index and Lane D adapter must preserve an explicit mode discriminator and prove byte-identical single-executor artifacts before old folders are deleted. Roll back by strata: advisor graph, commands, agents, reducers/adapters, governance and Lane D, never by whole-tree reset.
key_tradeoffs: One public skill ID simplifies advisor graph and docs, but demands stronger mode-discriminator tests because flat aliases can hide wrong-mode routing.
Promoting shared runtime helpers reduces duplication, but byte-identical artifact parity is more important than deduplication during this migration.
Keeping Lane D adapter-backed preserves the external contract, but means the merged workflow skill is intentionally not a universal host.
A unified governance root improves discoverability, but only if local IDs remain mode-qualified rather than globally flattened.
risks: Advisor routing collapses precision/recall when old skill IDs become one node without first-class mode metadata.
Reducer/helper path rewrites break byte-identical artifacts or resource-map emission.
Lane D external packaging contract drifts in env, argv, journal, kill-switch, lock or resume semantics.
Governance counts and CP IDs collide unless indexed by mode-qualified IDs.
Real native agent mirrors drift independently from symlinked command/skill dirs.
UNKNOWN-TARGET advisor edges are rejected as warnings unless post-scan gates fail closed.
Parallel-session worktree changes are accidentally staged or overwritten during large rename edits.
Per-mode optimizer lockedFields flatten and allow protected lifecycle fields to mutate.
open_questions: What exact persisted schema will carry the advisor mode discriminator for `deep-loop-workflows` results?
Will Lane D remain markdown-router-only, or is a new YAML workflow intentionally in scope as a separate behavior change?
Which normalized fields are allowed to differ in byte-identical artifact comparison besides timestamps/session IDs?
Can the skill-graph status/validate MCP transport be made reliable before migration, since the probe failed with connection closed?
Should `.codex/commands` be created, ignored, or explicitly documented absent in this checkout?
amendments_to_pass1: Amend iter-3: Lane D is currently a markdown command router with direct loop-host invocation, not a YAML asset workflow; preserve that surface unless a separate behavior change is approved.
Amend iter-9: expected advisor outputs must assert both `deep-loop-workflows` and a concrete mode discriminator; flat alias equality is not an adequate parity test.
Amend iter-7: represent `runtimeLoopType` as nullable/absent for improvement-derived modes and forbid inferring it from public `workflowMode`.
